author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU)
content:
MICU DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — UPMC
Date: 5/5/2027 (post-TIPS day ~2)

SUBJECTIVE/EVENTS: No further GI bleeding since the TIPS. However, over the past ~24-36 hours she has developed HIGH FEVERS (Tmax 39.5 C) and HYPOTENSION now requiring norepinephrine. She remains somnolent/unresponsive and is not waking despite weaning sedation.

OBJECTIVE: T 39.5, on norepinephrine, intubated. Exam without an obvious localizing source of infection; lines without overt site erythema; abdomen soft.

DATA: Hgb stable post-transfusion. Blood cultures drawn. (Workup in progress.)

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Fevers + new pressor requirement — presumed SEPSIS in a decompensated cirrhotic post-procedure. Broaden empiric antibiotics to VANCOMYCIN + MEROPENEM. Pan-culture (blood x2, urine); send repeat ascitic fluid; line evaluation. Continue norepinephrine.
#Persistent unresponsiveness — likely hepatic encephalopathy (TIPS-precipitated) vs sedation effect vs critical illness; optimize lactulose; weaning sedation; will reassess.
#Cirrhosis / post-variceal-bleed — continue prophylaxis course; serial labs.
(Working frame: occult infection driving the fevers/shock.)
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Lena Park, MD (MICU). 5/5/2027.
